ops.title: go-git v5 rejects extensions.worktreeConfig; cc-notes opens storage directly since v0.39.0
ops.body:
Decision + durable fact behind the v0.39.0 fix (commit ee53865).

go-git v5.17.0+ rejects opening any repo with extensions.worktreeConfig (also partialClone/preciousObjects at format v0) due to a case-mismatch bug in repository_extensions.go: extensions() lowercases names, but the extensionsValidForV0 allowlist keys are mixed-case, so the lookup never matches. Fixed upstream only on the v6 line (v6.0.0-alpha.4+); v5 backport PRs go-git#2171 / go-git#2167 were open and unmerged as of 2026-07-22. No v5 knob can skip the check — verifyExtensions runs inside git.Open on every open path.

cc-notes therefore never calls git.Open/PlainOpen: store resolves gitDir/commonDir via `git rev-parse --absolute-git-dir --git-common-dir` (gitcmd.Dirs), gitobj builds filesystem.NewStorage(dotgit.NewRepositoryFilesystem(...)) directly — verified byte-for-byte equivalent to what PlainOpen constructed — and verifyLayout (internal/gitobj/gitobj.go) rejects only layouts go-git truly cannot read: effective (last-value-wins) objectFormat != sha1, refStorage != files, unknown repositoryformatversion. Consequences: opening now requires git on PATH (like every other operation), and a fixed go-git v5.x release is a nice-to-have bump, not a revert trigger — the manual open is smaller and matches the gitobj/gitcmd split. Alternatives evaluated and rejected: go.mod replace (does not propagate to embedders of the public notes package), v6 alpha upgrade, git2go/libgit2 (binding unmaintained, pre-fix libgit2), exec-git plumbing rewrite (credible but strictly more work).
